summary: Full E2E verification of P0 (DB-backed catalog) + P1 (Stripe payment) + P2 (JWT/Google auth). Backend: 15/15 pytest passed (10 prior + 5 new: refresh, logout, brute-force lockout 429, Google session_token via Bearer, /orders/my for authed user). Frontend on https preview: home/shop/category/product detail all render 37 products from /api/products (Loading store splash clears). Register → /account → logout → re-login flow works (data-testids all present). Google login button present on /login (Continue with Google → google-login-btn). Stripe E2E: cart → checkout form filled → redirect to checkout.stripe.com → test card 4242 4242 4242 4242 → redirect back to /checkout/success → payment-success element visible → cart cleared → /account shows order with 'Paid' badge ($79.99 total). AI chat regression OK — gpt-5 streamed full Windows 11 Pro response. PayPal correctly shown disabled with 'Coming Soon'.

minor.issue: Stripe hosted page displays US$79.98 instead of US$79.99 (1¢ low). Root cause: float→cents conversion inside emergentintegrations StripeCheckout uses int(amount*100); int(79.99*100)=int(7998.9999...)=7998. Backend MongoDB order total is correct ($79.99). Fix: pass amount as Decimal, or pre-compute cents with round(amount*100) and pass that, or open issue against emergentintegrations. Verified by test_checkout_session_creates_order (total math is right server-side) but Stripe charged amount is 1¢ short per cart.

issues: GET /api/auth/me logs a 401 in browser console when guest visits any page (CatalogContext/AuthContext probe). Cosmetic — consider suppressing the network error log on guest probe or using a try/catch that swallows 401 silently.

Missing data-testid="order-number" on the success page (testid was requested in review). The order number IS rendered on /account 'My Orders' (e.g. #UC260611A6D6B6) and payment-success element is present. Minor — add an order-number testid to the success screen for automation.

action_items: (Minor, real $$ bug) Fix Stripe amount conversion so $79.99 charges as 7999 cents not 7998. Pass cents=round(total*100) or Decimal('79.99') into emergentintegrations.CheckoutSessionRequest.
(Cosmetic) Silence the guest /api/auth/me 401 console warning (try/catch + ignore in AuthContext).
critical_code_review_comments: payments.py uses float arithmetic for currency — switch to Decimal('79.99') or cents-int throughout to avoid 1¢ rounding (root cause of the Stripe $79.98 vs $79.99 finding).
routes/payments.py is well-structured (~187 lines): server-side price lookup + order creation before Stripe session is correct (cannot tamper price client-side).
auth.py brute-force lockout returning 429 after 5 failures works as designed (verified).
Google session validation accepts Bearer session_token and resolves via user_sessions collection — correct.
test_credentials: Admin admin@ucodesofttechus.com / Admin@UC2026! verified. Created E2E test users stripe_pay_*@test.com, orderchk_*@test.com, pytest_* (auto-cleaned in google session test, others remain).
seed_data_creation: Backend seeded 37 products on startup as designed. Test created inline user+user_sessions rows for Google auth test and cleaned them up. Stripe test order UC260611A6D6B6 left in DB as evidence of successful payment.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three phases (catalog migration, Stripe, auth) verified end-to-end including a real Stripe redirect + 4242 card payment + paid-status sync to /account. Only outstanding items are: (1) 1¢ float→cents bug in Stripe amount (charges $79.98 instead of $79.99 — fix in /app/backend/routes/payments.py line 113 or upstream), (2) missing testid on success page, (3) cosmetic console 401. Use https://software-staging.preview.emergentagent.com for browser tests (cookies are secure+samesite=none). Backend pytest: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v`. Both test files (test_api.py, test_auth_extra.py) are pytest and reusable.
rca of the issue: Stripe $0.01 underbilling: Python float 79.99*100 = 7998.9999999999999 (not 7999). emergentintegrations StripeCheckout.create_checkout_session converts to Stripe cents via int(amount*100) which truncates the .9999..., yielding 7998 cents. MongoDB order document keeps the correct $79.99 (rounded). Fix at app boundary: compute cents server-side with round() or Decimal and pass an integer cents value, or pass amount as Decimal('79.99'). Reproduction: cart with windows-11-pro qty 1 → checkout → Stripe displays US$79.98. Mitigation: see action item 1.
